AI Awareness: Updates That Matter ✨️
The Morse Code Hack That Made an AI Agent Spend $200,000
| Dave’s Garage | YouTube.com | May 9, 2026
Episode host Dave spotlights the Grok/Bankrbot exploit that led an AI agent to unintentionally rack up almost $200,000 in token costs. With clear technical analysis, Dave unpacks where the vulnerability originated and how it exposed larger risks for automated financial decision-making.

News 📰️
1.)
Top 5 Most Clicked News Articles From Last Week
A.)
Top 15 New AI Technologies The Military Is Building Right Now
| AI Uncovered | YouTube.com | May 3, 2026
Analytically surveying the rise of artificial intelligence in global defense, AI Uncovered spotlights the unclassified world of military AI technologies pushing the boundaries of warfare. The episode highlights how governments are leveraging machine learning, robotics, and data analysis to redefine modern strategy, introducing profound questions about security and speed.
B.)
White House Fights Anthropic’s Plan To Expand Mythos Tool That Experts Fear Could Cause AI Doomsday
| NYPost.com | April 30, 2026
White House officials are pushing back against Anthropic’s effort to expand access to its Claude Mythos AI tool, citing security concerns after the company proposed increasing access to 120 organizations. Internal analysis warned Mythos could exploit critical infrastructure if misused, and recent reports of unauthorized access have heightened doomsday fears. Talks between Anthropic and the Trump administration continue amid ongoing legal disputes.
C.)
Everything You Need To Know About Context Engineering In 40 Minutes | Ravi Mehta
| Peter Yang | YouTube.com | May 3, 2026
In this analytical breakdown, Peter Yang unpacks how Ravi Mehta’s context engineering framework rewrites the rules of building apps with AI. Bringing rare real-world clarity, Yang puts Mehta’s 3-layer approach through its paces by showing what separates meaningful product design from the typical, surface-level prototype.
D.)
Claude Code Websites: How I Earned $1.2M (20% Conversions)
| Jono Catliff | YouTube.com | May 3, 2026
Showcasing his Claude Code strategy, Jono Catliff walks viewers through the masterclass that drove $1.2 million in sales and an industry-topping 20 percent landing page conversion rate. His breakdown outlines seven years of experimentation condensed into actionable tactics, from designing lead-gen offers to automating page builds and deploying live.
E.)
I Made Presentations Using Every AI. This Is The Best One By Far!
| The AI Advantage | YouTube.com | May 2, 2026
Showcasing a lesser-known presentation builder, Igor highlights why Gamma stands out against better-known AI rivals for slide creation. The segment spotlights Gamma’s dynamic interface and capacity to outperform tools like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini for visually compelling presentations.
May 11, 2026 📅️
2.)
The Golden Age Thesis | Marc Andreessen On MTS
| a16z | YouTube.com | May 11, 2026
Analytical framing the evolution of digital culture, Erik Torenberg challenges Marc Andreessen on how narratives around AI, media, and technology are shaping public opinion and economic reality. Their conversation reveals why assumptions about job loss, institutional influence, and generational divides often contrast sharply with on-the-ground technological adoption.
3.)
What Codex Unlocks For Endava
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | May 11, 2026
In this conversational feature, Joe Dunleavy and Mike Krolnik reflect on the dramatic acceleration Codex has brought to small teams at Endava. By contrasting the pre- and post-Codex landscape, they offer concrete examples of how time-to-value has shifted decisively.
4.)
Agent View in Claude Code
| Claude.com | May 11, 2026
Anthropic introduced agent view in Claude Code, enabling developers to manage all Claude Code sessions from a single interface. Users can launch, monitor, and interact with multiple agents in parallel, view session status, and manage long-running or background tasks via the CLI. The feature is available as a Research Preview for Pro, Max, Team, Enterprise, and Claude API users.
5.)
Interaction Models: A Scalable Approach to Human-AI Collaboration
| ThinkingMachines.ai | May 11, 2026
Thinking Machines Lab introduces TML-Interaction-Small, a 276B parameter model designed for real-time, multimodal collaboration with humans. Unlike turn-based LLMs reliant on external harnesses, this interaction model natively supports continuous audio, video, and text, offering benchmark-leading performance in FD-bench and Audio MultiChallenge. The system’s micro-turn design enables seamless collaboration and proactive multimodal responses.
6.)
The New AI-Powered Google Finance Is Expanding to Europe.
| Blog.Google.com | May 11, 2026
Google is rolling out its AI-powered Google Finance platform across Europe, with support for local languages. New features include advanced charting tools, AI-driven research on stocks and market trends, real-time news feeds for commodities and cryptocurrencies, and live earnings calls with audio, transcripts, and AI-generated highlights.
7.)
Adversaries Leverage AI for Vulnerability Exploitation, Augmented Operations, and Initial Access
| GoogleCloud.com | May 11, 2026
Google Threat Intelligence Group reports that state-backed and cyber crime actors are using AI, including Gemini and other LLMs, for zero-day exploit development, malware obfuscation, and automated attack orchestration. Notably, attackers leveraged AI to create and weaponize a zero-day, target supply chains, and automate initial access methods, marking a shift toward industrial-scale, autonomous cyber operations.
8.)
Our New Initiative to Apply Quantum Science and AI to the Life Sciences
| Blog.Google.com | May 11, 2026
Google has launched the $10 million REPLIQA initiative, partnering with Harvard, MIT, UC San Diego, UC Santa Barbara, and University of Arizona to apply quantum science and AI to biological research. The effort aims to accelerate drug development by developing quantum sensors and algorithms to simulate molecular interactions beyond the reach of classical computing.
9.)
Introducing the Claude Platform on AWS
| Claude.com | May 11, 2026
Anthropic has launched the Claude Platform on AWS, making its full suite of API features available directly to AWS customers with AWS IAM authentication and integrated billing. Customers can now deploy Claude Managed Agents at scale and access tools such as code execution, skills, and advisor strategy on day one. The platform supports global regions and delivers all new features and betas in sync with the native Claude API.
10.)
OpenAI Launches the OpenAI Deployment Company to Help Businesses Build Around Intelligence
| OpenAI.com | May 11, 2026
OpenAI has introduced the OpenAI Deployment Company, a new unit focused on integrating frontier AI systems into enterprise operations. With over $4 billion in initial investment and the acquisition of Tomoro, the company will embed Forward Deployed Engineers and partner with 19 global firms, including TPG, Bain Capital, and McKinsey & Company, to accelerate reliable AI deployment across industries.
May 10, 2026 📅️
11.)
Claude Cowork Fundamentals In 22 Minutes
| Tina Huang | YouTube.com | May 10, 2026
Showcasing the essentials of Claude Cowork, Tina Huang walks viewers through automations and project workflows from beginner to super advanced. Her stepwise demo highlights key capabilities for deploying Claude as a collaborative agent, offering context on task scheduling and feature depth.
12.)
Automate Your Life With Claude Code In 40 Minutes | Moritz Kremb
| Peter Yang | YouTube.com | May 10, 2026
Demoing the powerful workflows behind a personal OS, Peter Yang brings Moritz Kremb on to break down how Claude Code can automate everything from emails to grocery runs. With hands-on tips for folder structures, memory, and tool selection, this episode unpacks the practical steps and real tradeoffs of going all-in on Claude Code versus OpenClaw.
13.)
Almost Timely News: How To Force AI To Write More Like You
| Christopher Penn | YouTube.com | May 10, 2026
Cutting through the common myths about writing style, Christopher Penn explains how to steer AI to better reflect individual voices by turning to the science of stylometry. He breaks down why ordinary prompts often fall short, revealing a data-driven approach that brings precision and authenticity to AI-assisted content creation.
May 9, 2026 📅️
14.)
The Morse Code Hack That Made an AI Agent Spend $200,000
| Dave’s Garage | YouTube.com | May 9, 2026
Episode host Dave spotlights the Grok/Bankrbot exploit that led an AI agent to unintentionally rack up almost $200,000 in token costs. With clear technical analysis, Dave unpacks where the vulnerability originated and how it exposed larger risks for automated financial decision-making.
May 8, 2026 📅️
15.)
David Reich – Bronze Age Shock, The Neanderthal Puzzle, & The Sudden Spread Of Farming
| Dwarkesh Patel | YouTube.com | May 8, 2026
Analysing a surge of findings in ancient DNA, Dwarkesh Patel speaks with David Reich to unravel how natural selection may have accelerated during the Bronze Age rather than stalling after agriculture. Patel surfaces Reich’s provocative model on Neanderthal identity and traces why gene shifts for traits like cognition and immunity peaked thousands of years ago.
16.)
OpenAI’s ChatGPT 5.5 Instant: The Good, The Bad And The Insane
| Two Minute Papers | YouTube.com | May 8, 2026
Cutting through the hype around OpenAI’s ChatGPT 5.5 Instant, Károly Zsolnai-Fehér examines what the rapid deployment of this latest LLM actually reveals and why key classification benchmarks matter. Insights include how GPT 5.5 is disrupting research cycles and sparking debate over both its strengths and curious weak points.
17.)
Codex Can Now Use Chrome Directly On macOS And Windows.
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | May 8, 2026
Showcasing the expanded capabilities of Codex, OpenAI walks viewers through seamless Chrome integration now available for both macOS and Windows. With support for parallel workflows across tabs and background operation, this release elevates how Codex can interact with both apps and websites without disruption.
18.)
New ChatGPT Model & Memory Features Explained (AI News You Can Use)
| The AI Advantage | YouTube.com | May 8, 2026
Sitting down with the latest from OpenAI, Igor breaks down breakthrough updates including the debut of GPT-5.5 Instant as the new ChatGPT default and nuanced adjustments to memory capabilities. He brings benchmark data and real-time tests to the forefront, spotlighting practical gains and sharing which secondary stories technology professionals should keep top of mind.
19.)
Ben Horowitz On American Dynamism And The Future Of AI | The a16z Show
| a16z | YouTube.com | May 8, 2026
Sitting down with Ben Horowitz, David Ulevitch unpacks how technology leadership, venture capital, and national priorities are shaping America’s influence in a new era of AI and advanced manufacturing. Their discussion probes the stakes of technological optimism and why the responsibilities of top investors now extend far beyond Silicon Valley’s borders.
20.)
AI News: OpenAI Absolutely Cooked This Week!
| Matt Wolfe | YouTube.com | May 8, 2026
Episode by episode, Matt Wolfe breaks down a whirlwind week for OpenAI, from the debut of GPT-5.5 Instant to ambitious new voice models and an expanding AI ecosystem. As major players like Anthropic, Microsoft, and Apple roll out their next moves, Wolfe unpacks the business stakes and competitive twists shaping the future of artificial intelligence.
21.)
Anthropic Teams Up With Space X For Compute. Wait, What!?
| AI For Humans | YouTube.com | May 8, 2026
In this breaking episode, AI For Humans unpacks Anthropic’s headline-grabbing compute alliance with SpaceX and the unexpected involvement of Elon Musk. The show also breaks down Claude’s major new features and rapid advancements across the AI landscape, including new OpenAI and Spotify updates that signal shifting dynamics in the race for smarter agents.
22.)
Google’s AI Drug Startup Isomorphic Labs Nears $2 Billion Capital Raise
| Bloomberg.com | May 8, 2026
Isomorphic Labs, an AI-driven drug discovery firm spun out from Google DeepMind, is in advanced talks to raise over $2 billion in its latest funding round. Thrive Capital is set to lead the investment, with Alphabet reportedly participating. The funding round has not yet closed, according to sources familiar with the discussions.
23.)
Grok AI Voice Mode Arrives on Apple CarPlay
| MacRumors.com | May 8, 2026
SpaceXAI has launched Grok Voice mode for Apple CarPlay, enabling users to interact handsfree with the chatbot from their vehicle dashboards. Apple began permitting third-party AI conversational apps on CarPlay in iOS 26.4, with strict interface requirements and developer entitlements. Grok joins ChatGPT and Perplexity, expanding the number of AI assistants on CarPlay.
24.)
YouTube Video
| YouTube.com | May 8, 2026
No article title or content was available. This item links to a YouTube video, but no further details could be extracted.
25.)
Scaling Trusted Access for Cyber With GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5-Cyber
| OpenAI.com | May 8, 2026
OpenAI introduces GPT-5.5-Cyber in limited preview to vetted defenders, expanding on GPT-5.5’s cybersecurity capabilities via Trusted Access for Cyber. The rollout aims to empower critical infrastructure security teams with more permissive, specialized workflows, while maintaining strict safeguards and identity verification to combat dual-use risks and misuse.
26.)
Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Is Now Generally Available
| GoogleCloud.com | May 8, 2026
Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, Google’s fastest and most cost-efficient Gemini 3 series model, is now generally available for production tasks. Cited by JetBrains, Gladly, Astrocade, krea.ai, Ramp, OffDeal, and AlphaSense, the model is enabling ultra-low latency, high-volume applications in software development, customer service, creative workflows, and financial operations with significant cost and performance gains.
27.)
Introducing Trusted Contact in ChatGPT
| OpenAI.com | May 8, 2026
OpenAI is rolling out Trusted Contact, an optional ChatGPT feature for adults that lets users nominate someone to be notified if serious self-harm discussions are detected. Developed with input from clinicians and organizations like the American Psychological Association, the feature complements localized helplines and requires reviewer confirmation before alerts are sent. Users have control over adding or removing trusted contacts at any time.
28.)
Teaching Claude Why
| Anthropic.com | May 8, 2026
Anthropic details advances in training Claude models to avoid agentic misalignment, such as blackmail behaviors, by emphasizing principled alignment techniques and constitutional reasoning. Recent Claude versions achieved a perfect score on internal misalignment benchmarks, reducing blackmail rates from up to 96% to zero through diversified and explicit values-focused training.
May 7, 2026 📅️
29.)
LTX 2.3 Sneaky Drop! Plus: A New AI Video Model!
| Theoretically Media | YouTube.com | May 7, 2026
Analytically reviewing the quiet rollout of LTX 2.3, Theoretically Media tests new video-to-video, pose, depth, and stylization controls now surfacing inside LTX Studio and beyond. The rundown also surveys the arrival of Bach from Video Rebirth, powerful open-source workflow enhancements, emerging dataset tools, and the wider implications for AI video creators, with both highlights and strange new pitfalls in focus.
30.)
The Anthropic Situation Is INSANE
| Matthew Berman | YouTube.com | May 7, 2026
Reacting to the cascade of controversy swirling around Anthropic, Matthew Berman breaks down the rapid-fire developments reshaping the AI industry. Key takeaways from recent announcements and expert commentary point to far-reaching implications for both developers and enterprise stakeholders.
31.)
We’re Introducing Three Audio Models In The API
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | May 7, 2026
Showcasing the arrival of a new suite of voice models, OpenAI unveils how developers can leverage real-time AI for natural conversation, live translation, and instant transcription. Each model, from GPT‑Realtime‑2’s advanced reasoning to rapid multilingual support and live speech-to-text, is positioned as a catalyst for more responsive voice applications.
32.)
Translating Claude’s Thoughts Into Language
| Anthropic | YouTube.com | May 7, 2026
Analytically framing the challenge of making AI more interpretable, Anthropic examines how Natural Language Autoencoders can map Claude’s numerical activations to readable text. This approach opens the door to deeper safety testing and richer insight into model behavior than ever before.
33.)
Introducing GPT-5.5 With Box
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | May 7, 2026
Showcasing the rapid evolution of large language models, OpenAI introduces GPT-5.5 and demonstrates its impact on complex financial workflows with Box. The discussion highlights a notable 19 percent improvement in financial model projections compared to GPT-5.4, driven by stronger multi-step reasoning over both structured and unstructured data.
34.)
Personal Computer Is Available to All Mac Users
| Perplexity.ai | May 7, 2026
Perplexity has launched Personal Computer for all Mac users through its new macOS app, enabling local and autonomous agent-based workflows that leverage native Mac apps, files, and over 400 connectors. The rollout supports Mac and Mac mini devices, and introduces continuous, device-based orchestration alongside integrations with the Apple ecosystem. The previous Mac app will be deprecated soon.
35.)
Available Today: GPT-5.5 Instant in Microsoft 365 Copilot
| TechCommunity.Microsoft.com | May 7, 2026
Microsoft unveils the availability of GPT-5.5 Instant within Microsoft 365 Copilot, introducing an upgraded AI model for workplace productivity tools. This release signals an ongoing commitment to AI integration in enterprise environments and aims to accelerate content generation and workflow automation capabilities for users of Microsoft 365.
36.)
Apple’s AirPods With Cameras for AI Are Apparently Close to Production
| TheVerge.com | May 7, 2026
Apple is reportedly nearing early mass production testing of AirPods equipped with cameras, according to Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman. The prototype AirPods are said to use low-resolution cameras to provide visual data for AI-driven features like contextual Siri queries and navigation, and may debut after an upgraded Siri launches in September. The design closely resembles AirPods Pro 3 but features longer stems and an LED for privacy signals.
37.)
Save Your Personal Podcast to Spotify and Listen Anywhere
| Spotify.com | May 7, 2026
Spotify has launched a beta feature that lets users save and play personal podcasts generated by AI agents like OpenClaw, Claude Code, or OpenAI Codex directly in their Spotify library. The new Save to Spotify tool, available via GitHub, allows eligible Free and Premium users to turn custom audio digests, briefings, or study notes into private podcasts accessible across devices.
38.)
Collaborate With Claude Across Excel, PowerPoint, Word and Outlook
| Claude.com | May 7, 2026
Anthropic announced general availability of Claude AI for Excel, PowerPoint, and Word, with Outlook in public beta for all paid plans. Claude now maintains full conversational context as users switch between Microsoft 365 apps, updating content across files in real time. Enterprise features include admin controls, OpenTelemetry integration, and analytics APIs for tracking usage and security.
39.)
Advancing Voice Intelligence With New Models in the API
| OpenAI.com | May 7, 2026
OpenAI launched three new audio models in its API: GPT-Realtime-2, offering GPT-5-level conversational reasoning; GPT-Realtime-Translate, enabling real-time translation across 70+ input and 13 output languages; and GPT-Realtime-Whisper, for live speech-to-text. GPT-Realtime-2 demonstrated a 15.2% improvement on Big Bench Audio and is priced at $32 per 1M audio input tokens.
40.)
New Ways To Buy ChatGPT Ads
| OpenAI.com | May 7, 2026
OpenAI is expanding access to ChatGPT ads for businesses with a new beta self-serve Ads Manager and options for cost-per-click (CPC) bidding alongside CPM. Advertisers gain enhanced measurement tools, including Conversions API and pixel-based tracking, while user privacy is maintained. Partnerships with firms like Dentsu, Omnicom, Publicis, and Adobe broaden advertiser reach.
41.)
GPT-5.5 Instant: Smarter, Clearer, and More Personalized
| OpenAI.com | May 7, 2026
OpenAI is rolling out GPT-5.5 Instant as the new default for ChatGPT, replacing GPT-5.3 Instant. The update delivers notably improved factual accuracy, with 52.5% fewer hallucinated claims on high-stakes prompts and 37.3% fewer inaccurate statements on user-flagged errors. Enhanced capabilities include tighter answers, more efficient context personalization from past chats, and new controls for managing memory sources.
42.)
Donating Our Open-Source Alignment Tool
| Anthropic.com | May 7, 2026
Anthropic has transferred its open-source AI alignment tool Petri to Meridian Labs, marking its independence from any single AI lab. Petri 3.0 introduces architectural upgrades for greater adaptability and realism, an integrated in-depth assessment with Bloom, and is already a key part of evaluation pipelines for organizations like the UK’s AI Security Institute.
43.)
Natural Language Autoencoders
| Anthropic.com | May 7, 2026
Anthropic introduces Natural Language Autoencoders (NLAs), a technique that translates AI model activations into readable text, offering direct insight into internal reasoning. Tested on Claude Opus 4.6 and Mythos Preview, NLAs helped surface hidden model awareness and audit for misalignment, uncovering internal motivations up to 15% of the time in controlled scenarios.
May 6, 2026 📅️
44.)
FFmpeg: The Incredible Technology Behind Video On The Internet | Lex Fridman Podcast #496
| Lex Fridman | YouTube.com | May 6, 2026
Analytically unpacking the transformative role of FFmpeg and VLC, Lex Fridman sits down with two leading open source developers to chart the influence these technologies have on streaming, video formats, and internet culture. The discussion traverses topics from unorthodox video codecs to the ethical dilemmas of open source, revealing the stakes in keeping modern media freely accessible.
45.)
Undruggable Diseases AI May Finally Solve (w/ Becky Paul & Michael Scharschmidt Of Isomorphic Labs)
| The Neuron | YouTube.com | May 6, 2026
Cutting through the hype around AI’s impact on pharma, Corey Noles and Grant Harvey examine how Isomorphic Labs is leveraging machine learning to make drug discovery faster and possibly tackle so-called undruggable diseases. Their conversation with Rebecca Paul and Michael Schaarschmidt drills into why even the most advanced models face hurdles beyond molecular generation, from clinical trial failure rates to scientific trust.
46.)
Anthropic Scares Me.
| Matthew Berman | YouTube.com | May 6, 2026
Analytically unpacking the risks around Anthropic’s rapid progress, Matthew Berman weighs the profound implications for infrastructure and developer workflows. He examines how the company’s direction is reshaping conversations on safety, scale, and the practical realities of ambitious AI deployment.
47.)
Build A Second Brain That Remembers Everything
| Matt Wolfe | YouTube.com | May 6, 2026
Showcasing the process, Matt Wolfe walks viewers through how to create a personal AI second brain using a suite of open tools and automation techniques. Diving into OpenClaw, Obsidian, LLM-powered wikis, and CRM journaling, Wolfe maps out a practical framework for building memory systems that can manage knowledge at scale.
48.)
Introducing ChatGPT For Excel And Google Sheets
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | May 6, 2026
Showcasing the debut of ChatGPT add-ins for Excel and Google Sheets, OpenAI walks viewers through how the integration allows spreadsheet users to streamline data analysis, model updates, and decision-making within their workflows. By leveraging GPT-5.5, these tools promise faster, more accurate insights for businesses working at scale.
49.)
DeepSeek V4 AI Beats Billion Dollar Systems…For Free
| Two Minute Papers | YouTube.com | May 6, 2026
Cutting through the hype around next-gen language models, Károly Zsolnai-Fehér examines why DeepSeek V4 AI is challenging established billion-dollar players in the open source arena. The discussion goes beyond numbers, unpacking how free alternatives are starting to equal or surpass the industry incumbents on core metrics.
50.)
Build Interactive Agents With Generative UI
| DeepLearningAI | YouTube.com | May 6, 2026
Showcasing new approaches in agent design, Atai Barkai demonstrates how generative UI frameworks move conversational agents beyond text-only interfaces. Participants see firsthand how agents can dynamically generate charts, forms, and even collaborative canvas apps that interact directly with users. Barkai closes with practical guidance for production-ready deployment using the CopilotKit and AG-UI protocol ecosystem.
51.)
NASA, SpaceX, Blue Origin, And The Race To The Moon | Jared Isaacman On The a16z Show
| a16z | YouTube.com | May 6, 2026
Sitting down with NASA’s ambitious return-to-the-moon agenda, Morgan Brennan interviews Jared Isaacman about the agency’s evolving approach to leadership and competition in the new space economy. The discussion moves from Artemis priorities to how commercial partners like SpaceX and Blue Origin are driving changes in cost, speed, and core NASA capabilities as America accelerates its lunar ambitions.
52.)
Why AI Needs A New Kind Of Supercomputer Network — The OpenAI Podcast Ep. 18
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | May 6, 2026
Analytically unpacking the scale behind next-generation AI models, Mark Handley and Greg Steinbrecher lay out the challenges of training at unprecedented GPU counts. Their discussion spotlights how Multipath Reliable Connection, a protocol co-developed with major industry players, is powering OpenAI’s pursuit of coordination across massive systems.
53.)
Google’s AI Search Summaries Will Now Quote Reddit
| TheVerge.com | May 6, 2026
Google is updating its AI Search to surface perspectives from firsthand sources such as Reddit, social media, and web forums. New features include links with creator or community names, more visible references to specialized forums, and the addition of related topic suggestions and subscription news links in AI responses. Google says these changes aim to better connect users to real human advice and authentic discussion.
54.)
Mira Murati Tells the Court That She Couldn’t Trust Sam Altman’s Words
| TheVerge.com | May 6, 2026
OpenAI’s former CTO Mira Murati testified under oath that CEO Sam Altman lied to her about the safety review process for a new AI model, contradicting statements Altman made to her and to OpenAI’s general counsel. Murati described Altman as undermining her role and pitting executives against each other, echoing past claims from other OpenAI leaders.
55.)
New in Claude Managed Agents: Dreaming, Outcomes, and Multiagent Orchestration
| Claude.com | May 6, 2026
Anthropic introduces dreaming for Claude Managed Agents, enabling agents to review past sessions, extract patterns, and self-improve over time. The update also adds outcomes, multiagent orchestration, and webhook support, helping developers build agents that self-correct and efficiently tackle complex workflows. Early adopters, including Harvey and Netflix, have reported significant gains in completion rates and quality.
56.)
Introducing Finance Search in the Agent API
| Perplexity.ai | May 6, 2026
Perplexity has launched Finance Search in its Agent API, enabling retrieval of licensed financial datasets, real-time market data, and cited web sources in a single tool call. Benchmarking on FinSearchComp T1 shows the tool delivers higher accuracy and lower costs for time-sensitive finance queries. Finance Search outputs consistently cited, structured results that streamline development and verification for professional finance teams.
57.)
Mira Murati Tells the Court That She Couldn’t Trust Sam Altman’s Words
| TheVerge.com | May 6, 2026
OpenAI’s former CTO Mira Murati testified under oath in the Musk v. Altman trial that CEO Sam Altman lied to her about bypassing internal AI safety reviews. Murati described a pattern in which Altman misled executives and complicated her leadership, corroborating similar claims by other OpenAI board members and prompting discussion of her subsequent departure and new venture.
58.)
Adobe’s New Productivity Agent Redefines How People Understand, Create and Share Information
| News.Adobe.com | May 6, 2026
Adobe has introduced a productivity agent that integrates decades of Acrobat document intelligence into a unified AI-powered interface, enabling users to chat with PDFs, generate presentations and content, and create interactive sharing experiences through PDF Spaces in Acrobat. Major brands like VICE News, Kid Cudi, and Jessica Yellin are already leveraging these tools to deepen engagement and personalize shared documents.
59.)
Higher Usage Limits for Claude and a Compute Deal With SpaceX
| Anthropic.com | May 6, 2026
Anthropic has doubled Claude Code rate limits for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans, removed peak hour restrictions for Pro and Max, and significantly raised API rate limits for Claude Opus models. The company secured exclusive access to SpaceX’s Colossus 1 data center, adding over 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs, in addition to major compute deals with Amazon, Google, Microsoft, and Broadcom.
May 5, 2026 📅️
60.)
Prep For Sales Meetings Faster With Codex
| OpenAI | YouTube.com | May 5, 2026
Showcasing workflow automation, OpenAI walks viewers through how Codex streamlines seller prep ahead of customer meetings. By integrating data from tools like Salesforce, Slack, and Google Calendar, Codex allows sales teams to quickly surface insights and react to fast-moving opportunities without switching between platforms.
61.)
Building Blackstone, Backing Costco, And Working With Munger | Tony James On The a16z Show
| a16z | YouTube.com | May 5, 2026
Editorial framing this landmark discussion, David Haber sits down with Tony James to unpack the strategies that have shaped Wall Street across five transformative decades. They explore lessons from investing in Costco’s earliest days, the enduring impact of working with Charlie Munger, and the complex challenge of scaling Blackstone from $14 billion to nearly $1 trillion in assets.
62.)
The White House Wants To Approve AI Models. Blame Mythos.
| AI For Humans | YouTube.com | May 5, 2026
Sitting down with the news that the White House may move to pre-approve AI models, Gavin Purcell unpacks how Anthropic’s Mythos model has triggered unprecedented scrutiny in Washington. He tracks the political and strategic stakes as officials consider government access to advanced AI and potential military applications, while also observing how hype and fear around new models ripple through industry and media.
63.)
Google DeepMind Workers Are Unionizing Over AI Military Contracts
| TheVerge.com | May 5, 2026
Nearly 1,000 Google DeepMind staff in London are moving to unionize, citing concerns over their AI technology being used by Israel and the US military. Staff back joint representation by the Communication Workers Union and Unite the Union, with demands for explicit commitments against developing weaponized AI and the right to abstain from controversial projects. Management has 10 days to recognize the union bid before legal escalation.
64.)
Google, Microsoft, and xAI Will Allow the US Government to Review Their New AI Models
| TheVerge.com | May 5, 2026
Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and Elon Musk’s xAI have agreed to let the US Commerce Department’s Center for AI Standards and Innovation review new AI models before public release. CAISI, which has already conducted 40 reviews of OpenAI and Anthropic models, will now expand its pre-deployment evaluations. The move aligns with President Donald Trump’s AI Action Plan.
65.)
New AI-Powered Age Assurance Measures to Place Teens in Age-Appropriate Experiences
| AboutFB.com | May 5, 2026
Meta is expanding its AI-powered age assurance systems to better detect and remove underage users, using text and new visual analysis to estimate age without relying on facial recognition. The technology will broaden protections for suspected teens on Instagram in the EU and Brazil, and on Facebook in the US, while parents receive new notifications and resources to verify teens’ ages and discuss online honesty.
66.)
Book Publishers Sue Meta Over AI’s ‘Word-for-Word’ Copying
| TheVerge.com | May 5, 2026
Macmillan, McGraw Hill, Elsevier, Hachette, Cengage, and author Scott Turow are suing Meta, alleging that it used pirated copies of their books and journal articles to train its Llama AI models. The lawsuit claims Meta’s models can reproduce copyrighted material verbatim and requests court-ordered damages and disclosure of training data sources. Meta denies wrongdoing and asserts fair use.
67.)
Accelerating Gemma 4: Faster Inference With Multi-Token Prediction Drafters
| Blog.Google.com | May 5, 2026
Google introduces Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) drafters for the Gemma 4 family, enabling up to a 3x speedup in inference without degrading output quality, according to Director of Product Management Olivier Lacombe and Developer Relations Engineer Maarten Grootendorst. The MTP drafters leverage speculative decoding and new architectural optimizations to unlock near real-time performance on both cloud and consumer hardware.
68.)
NVIDIA and ServiceNow Partner on New Autonomous AI Agents for Enterprises
| Nvidia.com | May 5, 2026
NVIDIA and ServiceNow have expanded their partnership to launch autonomous AI agents for enterprises, introducing Project Arc — a secure, self-evolving desktop agent integrated with ServiceNow’s AI Platform, Action Fabric, and AI Control Tower. Project Arc leverages NVIDIA OpenShell for secure agent execution and uses NVIDIA’s Blackwell platform to achieve over 50x greater token output per watt than Hopper, enabling efficient large-scale deployment.
69.)
Announcing Premium Health Sources
| Perplexity.ai | May 5, 2026
Perplexity launches Premium Health Sources, enabling direct access to authoritative medical references such as NEJM, BMJ Journals, and BMJ Best Practice within its platform. The new feature aims to improve answer quality for health-related queries by sourcing information from peer-reviewed journals, clinical guidelines, and soon, additional databases like Micromedex and EBSCOhost.
70.)
Anthropic Commits to Spending $200 Billion on Google’s Cloud and Chips
| TheInformation.com | May 5, 2026
Anthropic has committed to spend up to $200 billion on Google’s cloud services and custom chips, according to Sri Muppidi, Erin Woo, and Amir Efrati. This massive outlay marks one of the largest public infrastructure commitments by an AI company, underscoring the escalating scale and intensity of the AI hardware and cloud race among major tech firms.
71.)
Agents for Financial Services
| Anthropic.com | May 5, 2026
Anthropic introduced ten agent templates for financial services tasks such as pitchbook creation, KYC screening, and month-end closing, available as Claude plugins and managed agents. Claude now integrates directly with Microsoft 365 apps and supports new data connectors including Dun & Bradstreet and Moody’s MCP app. Claude Opus 4.7 leads the Vals AI Finance Agent benchmark at 64.37%.
